title: Mormolyca ringens
taxonomy: Mormolyca ringens is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Mormolyca, which is a group of orchids native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The genus Mormolyca is part of the subfamily Maxillarioideae, which is a subfamily of orchids characterized by their thick, leathery leaves and showy flowers. The Maxillarioideae subfamily is part of the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. This classification places Mormolyca ringens within a larger group of orchids that share similar characteristics and growth habits. The classification of Mormolyca ringens is an important tool for identifying and understanding the plant's relationships with other species.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal issues.
